meta.note: March 25 run — BREAKING: OpenAI kills Sora AI video generator (confirmed March 24, Guardian reported). Sprout Social Q1 2026: social media now #1 for breaking news, BUT 88% say AI video erodes trust — Guardian credibility is a structural advantage. DPS Day 3/final: Guardian AI reveal complete (recap due). Frame.io Q1 deadline 6 days. NAB 24 days with new journalism sessions announced. No new iconik or LucidLink releases.

decisionPanels.title: 💀 OpenAI Kills Sora — AI Video Market Restructures (March 24)
decisionPanels.summary: OpenAI announced on March 24 that it is shutting down Sora, its AI video generation app, just 15 months after launch and 3 months after signing a major deal with Disney for licensed character use. OpenAI said video-generation technologies will continue behind the scenes for robot training. This is a market-restructuring event: the best-known consumer AI video brand has failed commercially. Implications for Guardian: (1) GOVERNANCE: any evaluation of AI video tools (Schibsted Videofy, Runway, others) must include the question 'what is its commercial sustainability?' Sora had OpenAI's backing and Disney IP — it still failed. (2) CREDIBILITY ADVANTAGE: simultaneously, Sprout Social's Q1 2026 research (today) finds 88% of people say AI-generated video tools are eroding their trust in social media news. The Guardian's editorial-integrity approach to AI is now not just a values choice — it's an audience differentiation strategy. Publishers who do NOT slop AI video are gaining audience trust. (3) AI VIDEO GOVERNANCE NOTE: Schibsted Videofy (editorial review before publish) remains a sound model — human-in-the-loop AI video with editorial accountability is the distinction from 'AI slop'.
actions: READ Guardian's own coverage: theguardian.com/technology/2026/mar/24/openai-ai-video-sora — useful internal talking point about why not all AI video bets are safe.
USE in leadership conversations: OpenAI/Sora failure + 88% trust erosion from AI video = the case for Guardian's editorial-integrity AI strategy is now commercially validated, not just principled.

AUDIT any AI video tools under evaluation (Schibsted Videofy, Runway, etc.) against commercial sustainability. Prefer open-source (Videofy) or established infrastructure (AWS Elemental) over consumer AI video apps.

NOTE for MMR: Guardian's iconik metadata + editorial workflow is the infrastructure for human-accountable content. This differentiates Guardian from AI-slop publishers. Frame this in MMR go-live communications.

decisionPanels.title: 📱 Social Now #1 for Breaking News — But 88% Say AI Video Erodes Trust
decisionPanels.summary: Sprout Social Q1 2026 Pulse Survey (published today, March 25): social media has overtaken TV as the top source for breaking news. But the trust picture is nuanced and deeply important for Guardian's strategy: 88% of respondents say AI-generated video tools are eroding their trust in social media news. 56% frequently see 'AI slop' on their feeds. 66% are now more selective about engaging with social content. Yet 65% say their overall trust in social media stayed the same or increased. Gen Z: +25% YoY trust increase — and 51% of Gen Z explicitly want news organisations/reporters to be MORE active on social. 50% of Gen Z blocked, muted, or unfollowed a brand/creator due to AI slop. The strategic read: the audience is splitting. Guardian's credibility — real journalists, editorial accountability, explicit GenAI labelling (March 4 policy) — is a structural competitive advantage in this environment. The brands losing are the ones flooding feeds with AI slop. The winners will be the ones audiences can trust.
actions: BRIEF Guardian social/editorial team: social overtakes TV for breaking news. This validates the social-first strategy AND the investment in real journalist-as-creator content.
REFRAME Guardian Studios pitch to leadership: Guardian's editorial integrity IS the differentiator. Credibility is the product. 88% erosion in trust from AI video is the market opportunity.
TACTICAL: Guardian should lean into explicit journalist attribution on social content — bylines, faces, sourcing. This is what separates Guardian from AI slop in the audience's eye.
GEN Z SIGNAL: 51% want more news org/reporter presence on social. Guardian Studios journalist-as-creator model is exactly what Gen Z is asking for. Accelerate it.

decisionPanels.title: 🔴 Guardian AI Reveal — DPS Final Day Today (March 25), Recap Outstanding
decisionPanels.summary: The Digiday Publishing Summit is on its final day today (March 25, Vail CO). Chris Moran's presentation of the Guardian's first reader-facing AI feature happened March 23. As of this run, no detailed public recap of what the product actually IS has surfaced — the Digiday DPS page shows pre-reveal coverage only. The summit ends today. Expect full recap coverage to land on digiday.com/series/dps/ tomorrow (March 26) or Thursday. Separately: People Inc's CIO Jon Roberts gave a DPS session on AI licensing (Digiday covered today) — described OpenAI, Meta multi-year, and Microsoft pay-per-use deals. Aggressive bot-blocking + permissioned access underpins their marketplace. This is the peer-publisher commercial intelligence most relevant to Guardian/SPUR.
actions: CHECK digiday.com/series/dps/ Thursday morning (March 26) for Guardian AI reveal recap. THIS IS THE PRIORITY READ of the week.

READ today's Digiday: People Inc CIO Jon Roberts on AI licensing — OpenAI deal (multi-year access), Meta deal, Microsoft pay-per-use. letsdatascience.com/news/publishers-negotiate-ai-content-licensing-marketplaces-33f2ae48 for the detail.
KEY QUESTION when recap lands: what IS the product? Does it use video/multimedia archive? Is iconik a data source? If yes, MMR schema is directly in scope.
NOTE: Jon Roberts' framework (aggressive bot-blocking + permissioned access = foundation of licensing marketplace) is the most actionable AI licensing architecture described at DPS this year.

decisionPanels.title: ⚠️ Frame.io + LucidLink: 6 DAYS to Q1 Deadline
decisionPanels.summary: As of March 25 (today), the LucidLink + Frame.io integration remains in Technical Preview / waitlist status. Q1 2026 window closes in 6 days (March 31). No public GA confirmation has emerged. This integration enables Camera to Cloud → Camera to Timeline: field footage appears instantly in LucidLink Filespaces, editors cut directly in Premiere/After Effects. SEPARATE from LucidLink Connect (S3 streaming, GA for Enterprise) — do not conflate. The StorageNewsletter March 23 coverage of LucidLink Connect confirms the broader LucidLink story is strong, but Frame.io integration specifically remains unconfirmed.
actions: CONTACT LucidLink account team TODAY (6 days to Q1 close) — direct question: is Frame.io integration GA before March 31, or confirmed Q2?
If GA: activate for Guardian field teams immediately. Business + Enterprise plans.
If Q2: this won't land before NAB. Next window: post-NAB May. Plan accordingly. Confirm your plan tier includes it.
DO NOT conflate with LucidLink Connect (S3 streaming) — already GA for Enterprise, different use case.

decisionPanels.title: 🏭 NAB 2026: 24 Days — New Journalism Sessions Announced
decisionPanels.summary: NAB 2026 is April 18-22 (24 days). New journalism-specific sessions confirmed in the March 19 lineup announcement: (1) 'The Cost of Bearing Witness: Journalist Safety in a Polarized America' moderated by CNN anchor John Berman. (2) 'The Evolving Paradigm of Broadcast News' with Deborah Norville. (3) Creator Lab expanded (Adobe + Blackmagic Design partnership) with sessions on creator business, 'Beyond Views: Measuring Creator Impact', and 'Creator Survival Guide'. (4) Second AI Pavilion. (5) BBC Studios DTC CEO keynote. (6) Backlight at booth N2829. (7) CineSys demoing iconik + Twelve Labs + LucidLink. (8) Moments Lab agentic AI for video. (9) SMPTE VIBE AI governance (April 18 pre-show). The journalism-safety and broadcast evolution sessions add editorial relevance beyond the tech story — if attending, these are worth flagging to Guardian editorial leadership.
actions: DECISION required: NAB 2026 attendance. 24 days. Book accommodation now or it's gone.
If attending: minimum viable agenda = April 19-20, prioritise Backlight N2829 + CineSys iconik demo + SMPTE VIBE (April 18 pre-show if flying in early) + BBC Studios DTC CEO keynote.
PRE-SHOW: book meeting with Backlight account team in advance of NAB — ask for roadmap walkthrough.
REGISTER for SMPTE VIBE (April 18): AI-Driven Live Production + agentic AI governance session. Directly relevant to MMR AI framing.

decisionPanels.title: 🎬 Schibsted Videofy + AI Video Landscape Post-Sora
decisionPanels.summary: With Sora now shutting down, Schibsted Videofy's open-source model becomes even more strategically sound for Guardian evaluation. Videofy (open-sourced March 2026): AI converts published news articles to ready-to-publish news videos. Script generation, image/video matching, voiceover, final assembly — with editorial review before publish. Previously produced thousands of videos at Schibsted internally. Free, open-source, European peer-publisher tested. This human-in-the-loop model (AI assists, editors approve) is exactly what the Sprout Social trust data says audiences will reward: real editorial accountability, not AI slop. Could become a Project Launcher output type: article URL → video draft → editorial review → iconik collection.
actions: EVALUATE Schibsted Videofy: search github.com/schibsted for the Videofy repo. Free, developer resource needed to configure.
ASSESS fit with Guardian March 4 AI policy: GenAI labelling requirement. Videofy's editorial review step satisfies this — AI-assisted, not AI-generated without accountability.
GOVERNANCE framing post-Sora: distinguish Guardian's approach (editorial AI, human accountable) from commercial AI video apps. This is a reputational asset.
ROADMAP: add Videofy evaluation to Project Launcher roadmap as a potential article-to-video output type.

items.title: BREAKING: OpenAI shuts down Sora AI video generator — 15 months after launch, 3 months after Disney deal. Video tech to be used for robot training only.
Sprout Social Q1 2026: social media overtakes TV for breaking news. 88% say AI video erodes trust. 51% of Gen Z want more news org/reporter social presence.
People Inc CIO Jon Roberts at Digiday DPS: AI licensing deals with OpenAI, Meta, Microsoft. Bot-blocking + permissioned access = marketplace foundation.
NAB 2026 journalism sessions confirmed: journalist safety (CNN Berman), broadcast news evolution, Creator Lab expanded with Adobe + Blackmagic Design partnership
LucidLink Connect GA (Enterprise): instant S3 streaming, no migration — activate Guardian's existing S3 archives in WIP filespace TODAY
Frame.io + LucidLink: still waitlist/Technical Preview as of March 25. Q1 window closes March 31 (6 days). Contact account team urgently.
Iconik UI overhaul rolling March 2026: grey background, new navigation, Upload button removed — verify on Guardian instance, update training materials before go-live
No new iconik Web release confirmed (26.03 is latest) — next expected Q2 2026. Check Guardian instance for 26.03 feature availability.
Backlight 2026 Stats: audio +45% YoY — MMR iconik schema must include podcast/series/language/transcript/multi-channel audio from day one
CineSys demoing iconik + Twelve Labs at NAB 2026 — semantic AI video search without manual transcription. Book conversation ahead of NAB.
Guardian AI reveal at DPS (March 23): editor-trained non-chatbot. Recap expected tomorrow (March 26) at digiday.com/series/dps/
People Inc CIO Jon Roberts DPS session: AI licensing deals anatomy — OpenAI multi-year, Meta multi-year, Microsoft pay-per-use. Bot-blocking + permissioned access = foundation.
NMA + Bria AI: 2,200 publishers opt in to RAG revenue licensing. Guardian as SPUR co-founder should evaluate NMA model as SPUR implementation template.
The Atlantic 3-tier AI bot framework: Allow / Block / Yes-But — presented at DPS March 23. Guardian needs equivalent for SPUR.
Guardian mandatory all-staff AI training (March 4 policy): in-house tools — transcription, archive search, document analysis. iconik as API endpoint is the logical next step.
Schibsted Videofy open-sourced: AI converts news articles to ready-to-publish news video in minutes — free, peer-publisher tested, editorial review built in
Sprout Social Q1 2026: social overtakes TV for breaking news. 65% trust stable/rising. Gen Z +25% YoY trust. 51% want more journalists on social.
NAB 2026: 200% creator surge, second AI Pavilion, Creator Lab expanded (Adobe + Blackmagic), BBC Studios DTC CEO keynote — 24 days
Backlight 2026: vertical video +120% YoY — 9:16 must be first-class in MMR iconik schema and Project Launcher.
Meta Creator Fast Track: $1-3K/month to Facebook Reels creators — Guardian social team needs briefing on platform re-seeding
YouTube live control room vertical layout (March 13): zero-cost 9:16 from any horizontal live stream. Test on Guardian's next live event.
OpenAI shuts down Sora: 15 months after launch, 3 months after Disney deal. Video tech repurposed for robot training. AI video market restructuring.
88% of people say AI-generated video tools erode trust in social media news (Sprout Social Q1 2026). Guardian editorial integrity = structural competitive advantage.
Cloudflare compliant crawler: enforcement of AI content licensing at network layer. If Guardian uses Cloudflare, low-effort SPUR tool available today.
Moments Lab agentic AI at NAB 2026: multimodal video discovery, content search/creation/distribution automation
NMA + Bria AI: opt-in RAG revenue licensing for 2,200 publishers. SPUR co-founder Guardian should evaluate as implementation model.
People Inc AI licensing: multi-year deals with OpenAI and Meta, pay-per-use with Microsoft. Bot-blocking + permissioned access = the operating model.
